 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","title":"[FCP3/P1.3] Lock transition principles and guardrails against architectural backslide","description":"Background: migration work can silently backslide if contributors keep adding host local truth, compatibility wrappers, or new direct semantic edges while the refactor is in progress. V3 needs explicit anti regression rules, not just good intentions.

Scope: define guardrails for the transition period: no new compatibility first APIs, no new broad semantic buckets, no new fwc reach through into low level meaning, no new host only truth for facts that should be durable and evidence backed, and no new runtime compatibility debt that conflicts with the target architecture.

Acceptance: future implementation work has a clear list of prohibited shortcuts and review criteria for rejecting them.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:03.633363Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:08.259313Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:08.259087Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","governance","guardrails","mesh-first","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:19.678222Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2063,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-27: Published transition guardrails at docs/FCP3_Transition_Guardrails.md. Defines 8 guardrails (G1-G8): no broad buckets, no host-only platform truth, no fwc reach-through, no compat-first APIs, no untracked shims, no cross-layer deps, owner map enforcement, forbidden overlap enforcement. Includes PR review checklist.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hc2","title":"fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner","description":"# Scheduled Task Runner Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Time-based automation for FCP zones.

## Overview

Many automation workflows require scheduled execution: daily reports, periodic syncs, cleanup jobs, reminder systems. This meta-connector provides a robust scheduling infrastructure that triggers FCP actions on time-based schedules.

## Core Components

### 1. Cron Expression Scheduling
- Standard cron syntax (5-field and 6-field)
- Extended syntax with seconds precision
- Timezone-aware scheduling
- Named schedules for common patterns
- Cron expression validation and preview

```
# Standard patterns
@hourly    = 0 * * * *
@daily     = 0 0 * * *
@weekly    = 0 0 * * 0
@monthly   = 0 0 1 * *

# Custom patterns
*/15 * * * *     = Every 15 minutes
0 9 * * 1-5      = 9 AM on weekdays
0 0 1,15 * *     = 1st and 15th of month
```

### 2. One-Time Scheduled Tasks
- Schedule task for specific datetime
- Support for relative scheduling ("in 30 minutes")
- Cancellation before execution
- Rescheduling capability
- Batch scheduling (multiple one-time tasks)

### 3. Interval-Based Triggers
- Fixed interval execution (every N minutes/hours/days)
- Sliding window intervals
- Jitter support for load distribution
- Minimum interval enforcement
- Pause/resume capability

### 4. Task History and Logs
- Complete execution history
- Stdout/stderr capture
- Execution duration tracking
- Success/failure status
- Searchable log storage
- Log retention policies

### 5. Failure Alerting
- Configurable alert channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty)
- Alert on:
  - Task failure
  - Task timeout
  - Consecutive failures
  - Missed schedules
- Alert suppression/snooze
- Escalation policies

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[schedules]]
name = "daily-backup"
cron = "0 2 * * *"
timezone = "America/New_York"
action = "zone.backup.run_backup"
params = { full = true }
timeout = "1h"
alert_on_failure = true

[[schedules]]
name = "hourly-sync"
interval = "1h"
jitter = "5m"
action = "zone.sync.pull_updates"
retry_on_failure = 3

[[one_time]]
name = "migration-task"
run_at = "2024-02-01T03:00:00Z"
action = "zone.db.run_migration"
params = { version = "v2.0" }
```

## Task State Machine

```
PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
              ↓
           FAILED → RETRYING → COMPLETED/FAILED
              ↓
         DEAD (max retries exceeded)
```

## Dashboard Features

- Calendar view of scheduled tasks
- Next N upcoming executions
- Recent execution history
- Live execution monitoring
- Manual trigger capability
- Bulk operations (pause all, resume all)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Sub-second scheduling precision
- [ ] Support 1000+ concurrent schedules
- [ ] Missed task detection and alerting
- [ ] Distributed execution (no single point of failure)
- [ ] Complete audit trail
- [ ] Hot-reload of schedule configurations

## Dependencies

- Persistent storage for schedules and history
- Time synchronization (NTP)
- Alert channel integrations
- Zone action invocation system

## Considerations

- Handle daylight saving time transitions
- Cluster-safe (only one instance executes)
- Graceful shutdown (complete running tasks)
- Clock skew tolerance
- Long-running task support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:20.104073834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.225906099Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.225906099Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-hiu (fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-heeq","title":"Threshold Secrets (Shamir Secret Sharing)","description":"## Overview

Implement cryptographic threshold secret sharing using Shamir's Secret Sharing Scheme (SSS) over GF(2^8) as mandated by V2 spec §17.3. This is a DISTINCT system from RaptorQ symbol distribution - secrets use real cryptographic secret sharing because RaptorQ symbols can leak structure.

## Background & Justification

**Why This Matters:**
The V2 spec explicitly states: "Secrets use real cryptographic secret sharing (Shamir), not just RaptorQ symbols. RaptorQ symbols are NOT a secret sharing scheme—a single symbol can leak structure."

Key differences from RaptorQ:
- RaptorQ symbols can leak structure (not semantically secure)
- Single RaptorQ symbol may reveal partial information
- Shamir shares reveal NOTHING until k shares collected
- Wrapped shares ensure a node cannot decrypt other nodes' shares

**Design Philosophy:**
"Secrets are never complete anywhere" - the secret is only reconstructed ephemerally during use and immediately zeroized. No single device ever stores the complete secret.

## Normative Types (V2 Spec §17.3)

### SecretObject
```rust
pub struct SecretObject {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub secret_id: SecretId,        // Unique identifier
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,            // Zone binding
    pub k: u8,                      // Threshold (need k shares)
    pub n: u8,                      // Total shares distributed
    pub scheme: SecretSharingScheme,
    pub wrapped_shares: HashMap<TailscaleNodeId, Vec<u8>>,
    pub rotation: SecretRotationPolicy,
}
```

### SecretSharingScheme
```rust
pub enum SecretSharingScheme {
    ShamirGf256,  // Shamir's Secret Sharing over GF(2^8)
}
```

### SecretRotationPolicy
```rust
pub struct SecretRotationPolicy {
    pub rotate_after_secs: u64,    // Auto-rotate interval
    pub overlap_secs: u64,         // Both old and new valid
}
```

### SecretAccessToken
```rust
pub struct SecretAccessToken {
    pub jti: Uuid,                  // Unique token ID
    pub secret_id: SecretId,        // Which secret
    pub purpose: String,            // For audit trail
    pub requested_by: PrincipalId,  // Requester identity
    pub iat: u64,                   // Issued at
    pub exp: u64,                   // Expires at (short-lived!)
    pub signature: Signature,       // Approver signature
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. Shamir Secret Sharing Implementation
- Implement Shamir SSS over GF(2^8) with constant-time operations
- Support configurable k-of-n thresholds
- Each share MUST be wrapped (encrypted) so nodes cannot decrypt other nodes' shares
- Use per-node Tailscale keys for share wrapping

### 2. SecretObject Management
- Create/store secrets with proper threshold configuration
- Distribute wrapped shares to mesh peers
- Track share locations for reconstruction
- Implement zone-scoped access control

### 3. Secret Reconstruction (Ephemeral)
```rust
impl SecretObject {
    pub async fn use_secret<F, R>(&self, access_token: &SecretAccessToken, f: F) -> Result<R>
    where F: FnOnce(&[u8]) -> R
    {
        // 1. Verify access token
        access_token.verify()?;
        
        // 2. Create audit event BEFORE reconstruction
        self.audit_secret_access(access_token).await?;
        
        // 3. Collect k wrapped shares from peers
        let shares = self.collect_k_wrapped_shares(access_token).await?;
        
        // 4. Reconstruct using Shamir
        let secret = reconstruct_shamir_secure(&shares)?;
        
        // 5. Execute closure with ephemeral secret
        let result = f(&secret);
        
        // 6. Zeroize IMMEDIATELY after use
        secure_zero(secret);
        
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

### 4. SecretAccessToken Workflow
- Token issuance requires owner or delegated approver signature
- Tokens MUST be short-lived (minutes, not hours)
- Purpose field required for audit trail
- Verify before any secret operation

### 5. Secret Rotation
- Implement automatic rotation based on SecretRotationPolicy
- Support overlap period where both old and new secrets valid
- Coordinate rotation across mesh peers
- Maintain audit trail of rotation events

### 6. Integration with Credentials
The CredentialObject (§10.4) references secrets via SecretId:
- CredentialObject.secret_ref: Option<SecretId>
- Secret materialization requires SecretAccessToken
- Audit every secret access for credential injection

## Security Considerations

1. **Constant-time operations**: All Shamir math MUST be constant-time to prevent timing attacks
2. **Zeroization**: Use zeroize crate; secrets never persist in memory longer than closure execution
3. **Share isolation**: Wrapped shares use per-node keys; compromise of one node doesn't leak other shares
4. **Audit trail**: Every secret access creates AuditEvent BEFORE reconstruction
5. **Token validation**: SecretAccessToken signature and expiry verified before any operation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shamir GF(2^8) implementation with constant-time polynomial evaluation
- [ ] SecretObject with k-of-n threshold configuration
- [ ] Per-node share wrapping using Tailscale node keys
- [ ] SecretAccessToken creation and validation
- [ ] Ephemeral reconstruction with immediate zeroization
- [ ] SecretRotationPolicy with overlap period support
- [ ] Integration with CredentialObject for secret-backed credentials
- [ ] Comprehensive audit events for all secret operations
- [ ] Unit tests for Shamir math, threshold edge cases, rotation
- [ ] Integration tests for multi-node secret reconstruction

## Dependencies

This feature depends on:
- Core Types (ObjectHeader, ZoneId, etc.)
- Zone Architecture (zone-scoped access)
- Capability System (for SecretAccessToken authorization)
- MeshNode (for peer share collection)
- Observability (for audit events)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:41:27.758361051Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.445243106Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.445243106Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of 6o25.1 (enhanced with detailed V2 spec content)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-heeq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-heeq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-heeq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

This is the distribution pipeline that makes FCP installable by users outside this repository.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:29:43.948016Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2747,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose verification of .github/workflows/release.yml (2026-04-18):

WORKFLOW STRUCTURE — CORRECT:
1. Triggers: push tags v*, workflow_dispatch with version input ✓
2. Concurrency: group=release, cancel-in-progress=false ✓
3. Build matrix: 5 platforms (Linux x64+ARM64, macOS x64+ARM64, Windows x64) ✓
4. Packages: tar.xz (Unix), zip (Windows) ✓
5. Checksums: SHA256 per-artifact + combined SHA256SUMS.txt ✓
6. Signing: Sigstore cosign keyless (OIDC) ✓
7. Release: softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with download table ✓
8. Publish: dry-run only (actual crates.io publishes commented out) ✓

FINDINGS:
- Workflow has NEVER been triggered (gh run list --workflow=release.yml returns empty)
- No tags or releases exist in the repo
- Only 4 connectors built (anthropic, discord, openai, telegram) — ~146 others omitted
- fcp-host and fcp-cron binaries not included in release (only fwc CLI)
- Publish step is dry-run only — crates.io tokens not configured
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner may not be available in all GitHub tiers

CANNOT VERIFY WITHOUT TRIGGER:
- Whether the workflow actually succeeds on GitHub Actions (nightly toolchain + workspace deps)
- Whether cosign signing works with OIDC in this repo's configuration
- Whether the 45-minute timeout is sufficient for cross-compilation

RECOMMENDATION: Cannot close this bead without actually triggering the workflow. This requires creating a tag or using workflow_dispatch, which is a user action (pushing to the remote). The workflow definition appears structurally correct but is untested.","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:47:21Z"},{"id":2749,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose deep verification of release.yml (2026-04-19):

CRITICAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED (7f24fd41):
All 5 release targets (fwc, fcp-anthropic, fcp-discord, fcp-openai, fcp-telegram) depend on two external repos via local path:
- asupersync at ../../../asupersync (7 crates: fcp-async-core, fcp-host, fcp-telemetry, fcp-tailscale, fcp-graphql, discord, slack)
- toon_rust at ../../../toon_rust (fwc only, via 'tru' package)
These paths would not resolve on GitHub Actions CI. Fixed by adding shallow git clones in all 3 build jobs.

LOCAL VERIFICATION (macOS ARM64):
1. Release build fwc: OK (7.3 MB, 2m32s)
2. Release build fcp-anthropic: OK (4.0 MB)
3. Release build fcp-discord: OK (5.4 MB)
4. Release build fcp-openai: OK (4.1 MB)
5. Release build fcp-telegram: OK (4.9 MB)
6. tar.xz packaging: OK (5 archives, 1.5-3.1 MB each)
7. SHA256 checksums: OK (verified round-trip)
8. Combined SHA256SUMS.txt: OK

REMAINING ITEMS (cannot verify without triggering workflow):
- Cross-platform builds (Linux x64/ARM64, macOS x64, Windows x64)
- Cosign OIDC keyless signing (requires GitHub Actions OIDC provider)
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner availability (GA since Jan 2026)
- 45-minute timeout sufficiency for cold-cache builds
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 release creation

RECOMMENDATION: Workflow is now structurally ready for a test trigger via workflow_dispatch. +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","title":"[Registry] Verify GitHub Actions release workflow produces cross-platform binaries","description":"The .github/workflows/release.yml (11KB) exists but has never been demonstrated to produce artifacts. Verify: 1) Trigger the release workflow (manually or via tag), 2) Verify it builds for Linux x86_64 + aarch64, macOS x86_64 + aarch64, 3) Verify binaries are signed, 4) Verify checksums are generated, 5) Verify a GitHub Release is created with all artifacts attached. This is the distribution pipeline that makes FCP installable by users outside this repository.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:37:41.983827Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2747,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose verification of .github/workflows/release.yml (2026-04-18):

WORKFLOW STRUCTURE — CORRECT:
1. Triggers: push tags v*, workflow_dispatch with version input ✓
2. Concurrency: group=release, cancel-in-progress=false ✓
3. Build matrix: 5 platforms (Linux x64+ARM64, macOS x64+ARM64, Windows x64) ✓
4. Packages: tar.xz (Unix), zip (Windows) ✓
5. Checksums: SHA256 per-artifact + combined SHA256SUMS.txt ✓
6. Signing: Sigstore cosign keyless (OIDC) ✓
7. Release: softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with download table ✓
8. Publish: dry-run only (actual crates.io publishes commented out) ✓

FINDINGS:
- Workflow has NEVER been triggered (gh run list --workflow=release.yml returns empty)
- No tags or releases exist in the repo
- Only 4 connectors built (anthropic, discord, openai, telegram) — ~146 others omitted
- fcp-host and fcp-cron binaries not included in release (only fwc CLI)
- Publish step is dry-run only — crates.io tokens not configured
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner may not be available in all GitHub tiers

CANNOT VERIFY WITHOUT TRIGGER:
- Whether the workflow actually succeeds on GitHub Actions (nightly toolchain + workspace deps)
- Whether cosign signing works with OIDC in this repo's configuration
- Whether the 45-minute timeout is sufficient for cross-compilation

RECOMMENDATION: Cannot close this bead without actually triggering the workflow. This requires creating a tag or using workflow_dispatch, which is a user action (pushing to the remote). The workflow definition appears structurally correct but is untested.","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:47:21Z"},{"id":2749,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose deep verification of release.yml (2026-04-19):

CRITICAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED (7f24fd41):
All 5 release targets (fwc, fcp-anthropic, fcp-discord, fcp-openai, fcp-telegram) depend on two external repos via local path:
- asupersync at ../../../asupersync (7 crates: fcp-async-core, fcp-host, fcp-telemetry, fcp-tailscale, fcp-graphql, discord, slack)
- toon_rust at ../../../toon_rust (fwc only, via 'tru' package)
These paths would not resolve on GitHub Actions CI. Fixed by adding shallow git clones in all 3 build jobs.

LOCAL VERIFICATION (macOS ARM64):
1. Release build fwc: OK (7.3 MB, 2m32s)
2. Release build fcp-anthropic: OK (4.0 MB)
3. Release build fcp-discord: OK (5.4 MB)
4. Release build fcp-openai: OK (4.1 MB)
5. Release build fcp-telegram: OK (4.9 MB)
6. tar.xz packaging: OK (5 archives, 1.5-3.1 MB each)
7. SHA256 checksums: OK (verified round-trip)
8. Combined SHA256SUMS.txt: OK

REMAINING ITEMS (cannot verify without triggering workflow):
- Cross-platform builds (Linux x64/ARM64, macOS x64, Windows x64)
- Cosign OIDC keyless signing (requires GitHub Actions OIDC provider)
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner availability (GA since Jan 2026)
- 45-minute timeout sufficiency for cold-cache builds
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 release creation

RECOMMENDATION: Workflow is now structurally ready for a test trigger via workflow_dispatch. The user should run: gh workflow run release.yml -f version=0.0.1-test","created_at":"2026-04-19T03:18:03Z"},{"id":2759,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Handing back on 2026-04-19: latest GitHub Actions release run is still the stale queued run 24631366023 on commit 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3 (queued		Release	Release	main	workflow_dispatch	24631366023	46m45s	2026-04-19T14:27:42Z).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.5","title":"[Registry] Test supply chain rejection: tampered binary, expired signature, wrong platform all caught","description":"Negative tests for supply chain verification: 1) Flip a byte in signed binary -> install MUST fail with signature verification error, 2) Sign with expired timestamp -> install MUST fail with expired attestation error, 3) Present wrong platform binary (linux on macOS) -> install MUST fail with platform mismatch, 4) Present binary with capabilities not in zone ceiling -> install MUST fail with capability subset violation, 5) All failures should produce structured errors (not panics or generic messages). These prove the README's supply chain verification pipeline actually CATCHES attacks, not just that it verifies valid packages.","notes":"Progress 2026-04-07: strengthened negative coverage to assert structured errors for tampered binary, wrong platform, capability ceiling violation, SLSA insufficiency, missing attestation evidence, and untrusted builder; added explicit expired-attestation rejection path in fcp-registry via AttestationEvidence.expires_at + RegistryError::AttestationExpired, plus integration and unit tests for expiry; MockTufVerifier now also rejects expired TUF metadata. Fresh rch cargo check/test runs are in flight against the updated code.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:11.666886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T06:12:07.078783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T06:12:07.078553Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:11.666886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:12.350098Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Also added 14 tests to types.rs in previous session. Total: 45 audit tests passing (was 10).","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:45:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","title":"[FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors","description":"# [FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors

## Goal
Build a library of production connectors that **fully comply** with the FCP V2 platform:
- single-zone binding
- default deny via capabilities
- revocation freshness enforcement
- correct receipts and audit events
- sandbox + network constraints
- no secrets on disk; no secret logging

This epic intentionally assumes the **core platform** exists first.

## Entry criteria (what must exist before connectors are real)
Connectors should not begin until these are available:
- `[FCP2] Connector SDK (Rust)`
- `[FCP2] Connector Manifest (TOML)`
- `[FCP2] Policy Engine` (DecisionReceipts + reason codes)
- `[FCP2] Sandbox + Egress Proxy`
- `[FCP2] MeshNode` (enforcement boundary)
- `[FCP2] Registry + Supply Chain` (install/verify)
- `[FCP2] Conformance` (golden vectors + fuzz + compliance runner)

## Definition of Done
- Each connector has:
  - a manifest with explicit capabilities + sandbox profile
  - a conformance test suite (unit + integration with mocks)
  - E2E compliance run output
  - performance checks where applicable
  - no real external API calls in unit tests

## Notes
- Backwards compatibility is not a goal. Implement the V2 model directly.
- Mechanical compliance checking is part of the conformance suite.

## Success Criteria
- Every connector epic follows `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5` (base beads `.1`..`.4`, state model, NetworkConstraints, etc.).
- Every connector has mock-only unit/integration coverage per `flywheel_connectors-h32`.
- Tier-1 connectors (Telegram, Discord, OpenAI, Anthropic) each have:
  - a complete manifest/capability map
  - provisioning automation (where applicable)
  - passing E2E compliance runs (`flywheel_connectors-e3i9` + `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`).
- No connector requires hard-coded pricing tables; usage metrics are emitted and policy can compute costs externally.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","title":"[MOR/C3.5] Change default simulate() to deny; fix default-deny violation","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:57.765589Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:36:36.489235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:37.610429Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","default-deny","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:07.503486Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2644,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Change the default implementation of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait from returning SimulateResponse::allowed(req.id) to returning SimulateResponse::denied(req.id, reason: operation_not_simulated). WHY: J1 Deontic found this is a DIRECT VIOLATION of the default-deny principle. A connector that does not override simulate() currently implicitly claims ALL operations are allowed. This means a new connector scaffold that ships without implementing simulate() silently tells the system it can handle anything. The correct default is: deny everything the connector has not explicitly declared it can simulate. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/connector.rs or crates/fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs — the default impl of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait. Also update fwc new_cmd.rs scaffold template to include a simulate() override with proper operation matching. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Default simulate() returns denied. (2) Existing connectors that rely on default must be updated (or explicitly override with allowed for their operations). (3) fwc new scaffold includes simulate() override template. (4) At least 3 tests: default returns denied, override returns allowed, denied includes reason code. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day for the default change + 0.5 day per connector that needs updating. Quick audit needed: how many connectors rely on the default simulate()? DESIGN NOTE: This is a one-line change with outsized security impact. It converts simulate() from opt-out safety (dangerous default) to opt-in safety (safe default). Any connector that was relying on the old default was ALREADY WRONG — it was claiming support for operations it had not validated.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:50Z"},{"id":2652,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified complete: default simulate() returns SimulateResponse::denied(FCP-3010) since connector.rs:81-89. Scaffold template in new_cmd.rs:2126 has full simulate() override with operation validation, state checks, and capability verification. protocol.rs has 3+ SimulateResponse tests (allowed, denied with reason/code, with_cost). 44 connectors override simulate() explicitly; remaining ~46 correctly use default-deny. All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:16Z"},{"id":2653,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Changed default simulate() from allowed→denied (FCP-3010). Fixed pre-existing phantom type compile errors. 4 new tests, 3898 lib tests pass, clippy clean. Commit 0ce30890.","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:02:45Z"},{"id":2657,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.5 Default simulate() to deny — already implemented.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pdgc","title":"TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for configuration validation and schema framework.

## Test Categories

### Schema Validation
- [ ] JSON Schema validation
- [ ] Required field enforcement
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Format validation (URL, email, etc.)
- [ ] Custom validators

### Configuration Loading
- [ ] TOML parsing
- [ ] JSON parsing
- [ ] Environment variable expansion
- [ ] File path resolution
- [ ] Default value handling

### Secret Reference Resolution
- [ ] Secret placeholder parsing
- [ ] Vault integration mocking
- [ ] Environment variable secrets
- [ ] File-based secrets

### Validation Errors
- [ ] Clear error messages
- [ ] Path to invalid field
- [ ] Suggested fixes
- [ ] Multiple error collection

### Configuration Merging
- [ ] Override precedence
- [ ] Deep merge behavior
- [ ] Array handling

## Coverage Target: >95%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:48.109383666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","close_reason":"Superseded - parent bead fcp-config is closed. Github connector has scope-limited fix; broader connector OAuth helpers should migrate once the conformance harness blocker at crates/fcp-conformance/src/harness.rs:861 is resolved.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:45:33.342440Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:37:30.411849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2781,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pizld","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: releasing claim — collision with parallel in-flight work. fcp-oauth/src/redirect.rs (validate_registered_redirect_uri / validate_callback_redirect_uri / is_allowlisted / ensure_registered_redirect_allowlisted, plus OAuthError::InvalidRedirectUri + RedirectUriNotAllowlisted variants) is uncommitted but already in another agent's tree, while connectors/github/src/connector.rs has an uncommitted diff that imports ensure_allowlisted_redirect_uri / ensure_callback_redirect_is_allowlisted / normalize_registered_redirect_uri (different API). Whoever finishes pizld needs to pick one API and reconcile. Dropping back to open. Also: I created crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs as a parallel artifact before noticing redirect.rs existed — it is NOT wired into lib.rs (no mod declaration), so it does not affect the build, but should be removed by whoever owns the next commit on this bead.","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:37:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","title":"[FCP3/P6.4] Rewrite docs, examples, and transcripts so mesh backed operation is taught as normal","description":"Background: documentation and transcripts shape how future contributors think about the architecture. If they keep teaching host first operation as the normal model, the implementation will drift back toward that shape even after code changes land.

Scope: rewrite docs, examples, and operator transcripts so mesh backed operation is taught as the normal steady state, with host first truthfulness described only as a transition constraint or limited fallback case where appropriate. Derive the primary examples and command transcripts from scripted acceptance and end to end scenarios wherever possible so the teaching material reflects verified behavior instead of hand written intent.

Acceptance: a new contributor can learn the intended architecture from repo local docs without needing the original migration narrative, and the documented examples and transcripts correspond to tested flows with detailed logs and reproducible artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:08.557863Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:08:00.147118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:08:00.146875Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-6","teaching"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:13:11.847295Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kpj16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:50.971577Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:51.720502Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2596,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: All 3 children closed (pl7pj.1 README rewrite, pl7pj.2 deployment guide, pl7pj.3 scorecard reconciliation). Docs now teach mesh-backed operation as steady-state with host-first as transition constraint. Scorecard updated to 62% migrated with proof artifact citations.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:07:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj.1","title":"[FCP3/P6.4] Rewrite README and architecture examples around mesh-backed steady-state operation","description":"Background: documentation drift is one of the easiest ways to undo an architectural migration. If README examples and architecture explanations keep teaching host-first operation as normal, the codebase will drift back toward that model even after the runtime changes.

Scope: rewrite the primary README and architecture-facing examples so mesh-backed operation is taught as the intended steady state, with host-first truthfulness described only as a transition or fallback case when that is still genuinely true. Derive the main examples from acceptance artifacts wherever possible.

Acceptance: a new contributor can learn the intended mesh-first model from repo-local docs without needing the original migration thread, and the examples align with tested flows rather than with hand-written intent.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMill","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:57.288504Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:07:59.017803Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T16:07:59.017621Z","close_reason":"README and architecture examples now teach mesh-backed steady-state operation first, with host-first truthfulness framed as the transition control plane","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:45.667238Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kpj16.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:46.263866Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:57.288504Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2419,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group docs note: these beads should derive examples, runbooks, and teaching material from proof artifacts rather than hand-written optimism. Each surviving doc surface should cite the specific acceptance, transcript, or benchmark bundle it teaches so future contributors can trace every claim back to executable evidence.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:14Z"}]}
